c74object.name: tap.tune~
c74object.description: The tap.tune~ object is the classic retune effect: it detects the pitch of a monophonic input (voice or instrument), snaps it to the nearest allowed note, and glides the correction in at a settable retune speed. Slow speeds correct transparently while letting vibrato through; a speed of zero snaps instantly — the well-known hard-quantize vocal effect. Allowed notes come from a key and scale (with individual per-note enables via the notes message), or in midi mode from whatever MIDI notes are currently held (sent as note messages). The detector is a YIN-family pitch tracker; resynthesis is a period-locked granular transposer sharing its engine with tap.shift~. The object is monophonic by design — pitch detection needs a single voice. For multichannel signals, wrap it in an mc. operator.

method.name: notes
method.description: Set the allowed notes directly, as absolute pitch classes, replacing the key/scale derivation. Send either twelve 0/1 toggles (C through B) or a list of the enabled pitch classes (0 = C .. 11 = B).

method.name: note
method.description: MIDI note-on/off for midi mode: a pitch (0-127) and a velocity. Velocity 0 releases the note; anything else holds it as a correction target.

method.name: clear
method.description: Reset the audio state (buffers and glides).

attribute.name: key
attribute.description: Key root for the scale (c, c#, d .. b). Setting it re-derives the allowed notes from key + scale, replacing any custom notes mask.

attribute.name: scale
attribute.description: Scale the corrector snaps to, relative to the key: chromatic, major, minor, harmonic, melodic, pentatonic, or minorpentatonic.

attribute.name: mode
attribute.description: Target selection: scale snaps to the key/scale notes; midi snaps to the nearest held MIDI note and leaves the signal untouched when none are held.

attribute.name: speed
attribute.description: Retune speed in milliseconds — the time constant of the glide onto the target note. 0 snaps instantly (the hard quantize effect).

attribute.name: amount
attribute.description: Correction depth in percent (0-100). 100 lands on the target; 50 corrects half the distance.

attribute.name: minfreq
attribute.description: Lowest input frequency (Hz) treated as pitched.

attribute.name: maxfreq
attribute.description: Highest input frequency (Hz) treated as pitched.
